feed_metadata.title: KKME — Lithuanian BESS Regulatory Monitor
feed_metadata.description: Weekly regulatory updates relevant to Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) procurement, operation and grid connection in Lithuania, plus EU-level battery and grid code activity.

impact_levels.high: Material change to permit lifecycle, security instruments, technical requirements, liability or sanctions. Action recommended.
impact_levels.medium: Affects pricing, reporting, or timelines for in-flight deals. Worth a read.
impact_levels.low: Editorial, informational, or affecting deal types KKME does not currently advise on.

items.title: New Litgrid PETA — transmission network use procedures in force from 28 Feb 2026
items.title_lt: Pasinaudojimo elektros perdavimo tinklais tvarkos aprašas — galioja nuo 2026-02-28
items.summary: VERT (the Lithuanian energy regulator) approved a new edition of the rules governing how generation and storage assets connect to and use the Litgrid transmission network. The act sets the procedure description for transmission grid use, including connection requirements for power generation facilities and energy storage devices. Projects that were scoped before 28 Feb 2026 should be checked against the new procedure — connection-stage security obligations and technical compliance evidence formats can change between editions.

items.title: Updated ESO PETA — distribution network use procedures in force from 25 Nov 2025
items.title_lt: Pasinaudojimo elektros skirstomaisiais tinklais tvarkos aprašas — galioja nuo 2025-11-25
items.summary: VERT issued an updated edition of the procedure description for using the ESO distribution network. Covers connection rules for generation and storage devices on the DSO side. Relevant for sub-110 kV BESS sub-projects connected through ESO. Projects scoped against the previous edition should be re-verified for updated connection conditions and security requirements.

items.title: Electricity Law amendment XV-687 — consolidated version effective 1 April 2026
items.title_lt: LR Elektros energetikos įstatymo pakeitimas XV-687 — aktuali redakcija nuo 2026-04-01
items.summary: Latest substantive amendment to Lithuania's primary electricity statute (VIII-1881). The current consolidated version is in force from 1 April 2026. Any contract or technical document citing specific articles of the Electricity Law should be re-checked against current numbering before signing.

items.title: Energy Law amendment XV-688 — consolidated version effective 1 January 2026
items.title_lt: LR Energetikos įstatymo pakeitimas XV-688 — aktuali redakcija nuo 2026-01-01
items.summary: Recent amendment to the framework Energy Law (IX-884) covering general energy sector governance. Current consolidated version effective from 1 January 2026 to 31 May 2026. No immediate operational impact on running BESS deals identified, but worth a read for general framework references.

items.title: Civil Code Book VI amendment XV-779 — effective 1 April 2026
items.title_lt: LR CK VI knygos pakeitimas XV-779 — galioja nuo 2026-04-01
items.summary: Civil Code Book VI (law of obligations) was amended on 26 March 2026. Book VI is the primary contractual reference for force majeure, warranty, and security-of-obligations (prievolių užtikrinimas) provisions used in BESS supply agreements. Negligible direct impact expected on standard supply contract templates, but flagged because the change is recent.

items.title: Renewables Law (AIEI) amendment XV-560 — consolidated version effective 1 January 2026
items.title_lt: LR Atsinaujinančių išteklių energetikos įstatymo pakeitimas XV-560
items.summary: Latest amendment to the Renewable Energy Sources Law (XI-1375). Current consolidated version effective from 1 January 2026. Relevant for hybrid PV+BESS projects and any subsidy or auction interaction.

items.title: VERT updated electricity network connection tariff methodology for large non-household customers
items.title_lt: VERT atnaujino elektros tinklo prijungimo tarifų metodiką stambiems ne buitiniams vartotojams
items.summary: VERT updated the methodology for calculating network connection tariffs for large non-household projects. Affects connection cost calculations for new BESS and generation project applications. Projects with connection terms already issued under the previous methodology are unaffected.

items.title: EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542 — general info and capacity labels deadline 18 Aug 2026
items.summary: Article in EU Regulation 2023/1542 sets 18 August 2026 (or 18 months after the underlying implementing act enters into force, whichever is later) as the date from which batteries must carry general information labels, and rechargeable portable, LMT and SLI batteries must carry capacity labels. Applies primarily upstream to cell and module manufacturers; system integrators should verify that suppliers' delivered units are compliant.
